allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Well past peak — open with curiosity. May show tertiary earthiness or be fading. Cook with it if needed.

allWeeks.name: Stangeland Gewürztraminer
allWeeks.note: Two decades past peak. Tropical oxidation or maderization likely. Worth the experience.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Thibert Pouilly-Fuissé Vignes Blanches
allWeeks.note: Decade-plus past window. Oxidized white Burgundy can surprise — nutty, honeyed — or disappoint. Open with dinner.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: 8 years past peak. May still show secondary complexity — dried fruit, forest floor, light tannin.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: 4 years past peak. Likely still pleasurable — should show tertiary complexity.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Rosé of Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: 3 years past window. Rosé fades quickly — drink paired with a light meal and manage expectations.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Édition Rosé
allWeeks.note: Past window but a quality producer — may still be pleasant. Chill well.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Édition Limitée
allWeeks.note: CT94.5 and window closes 2027 — highest-rated urgent bottle in the cellar. Decant 30 minutes.

allWeeks.name: Campo Viejo Tempranillo Rioja Rosado
allWeeks.note: At the end of its window. Drink this spring before it fades further.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Counoise Ruby Ruth
allWeeks.note: At the tail of its window. Lovely for a warm late-May evening.

allWeeks.name: Whispering Angel
allWeeks.note: Classic summer Provence rosé. Peak now.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Gris
allWeeks.note: End of window — open now before summer heat pushes it further.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Rosé of Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Past its recommended window. Drink now while summer makes it worth it.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Gris
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. Light and refreshing for summer.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Bijoux
allWeeks.note: Sémillon-Sauvignon Blanc blend past its window. Likely still good — waxy, honeyed texture.

allWeeks.name: Veuve Clicquot Champagne Brut Vintage
allWeeks.note: In peak window. Perfect for a summer celebration.

allWeeks.name: Les Quatre Tours Vilandria
allWeeks.note: Peak drinking. A more complex, food-friendly Provence alternative.

allWeeks.name: Fratelli Pighin Pinot Grigio
allWeeks.note: Light, crisp Friulian Pinot Grigio — ideal summer patio wine.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Albariño Philips Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Citrusy and saline — great with seafood.

allWeeks.name: Stoller Pinot Noir Rosé
allWeeks.note: Peak now. Oregon rosé with more body — pairs beautifully with grilled salmon.

allWeeks.name: Fratelli Pighin Pinot Grigio
allWeeks.note: Second of two — perfectly at home in August.

allWeeks.name: Marqués de Cáceres Rioja Rosado
allWeeks.note: Enters drinking window now. Tempranillo rosé — more structured than Provence.

allWeeks.name: Ridgecrest Grüner Veltliner
allWeeks.note: End of its window. Grüner pairs perfectly with late-summer vegetables.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Tempranillo
allWeeks.note: Past its window. Open for a late-summer dinner — may still show earthy, dark fruit.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Lost and Found Rosé
allWeeks.note: 10-year-old rosé blend. Could be fascinating or faded. First of three — try one now.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: 3 years past window. First of two — drink now.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: 2 years past window. First of two. Likely still showing well.

allWeeks.name: Cantina Zaccagnini Montepulciano d'Abruzzo
allWeeks.note: End of window this year. Rustic and hearty — great with autumn pasta.

allWeeks.name: Cecilia Beretta Bardolino Classico
allWeeks.note: Lighter Italian red — Bardolino drinks young and fresh. Good autumn weeknight bottle.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Nalin
allWeeks.note: In its window now. Red Bordeaux blend — good for fall evenings.

allWeeks.name: Hedges Family Estate Red Mountain
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Red Bordeaux blend from Washington's top AVA — brooding and structured.

allWeeks.name: The Feminist Party GSM
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Grenache-Syrah-Mourvèdre — spice and dark fruit. Decant 30 min.

allWeeks.name: Tertre du Moulin St. Émilion Grand Cru
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Right Bank Bordeaux with Merlot richness — great with autumn roasts.

allWeeks.name: Reynvaan Syrah In the Rocks
allWeeks.note: Entering peak now. Walla Walla Syrah at its most mineral — decant well.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Syrah Artisan
allWeeks.note: In window. CT92 — first of two 2019 Artisan Syrahs. Compare with the 2020 later.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Laurène
allWeeks.note: Evolution track — Laurène Year 1. 2018 in peak window. 7 bottles remain; open one to benchmark.

allWeeks.name: Reynvaan Family Vineyards Syrah In the Hills
allWeeks.note: Peak and highly rated. Reynvaan's flagship — pair with roasted lamb or short ribs.

allWeeks.name: Barreri & Rovati Barolo Riserva
allWeeks.note: End of window approaching. 14-year-old Barolo Riserva — tertiary notes of tar, rose, leather.

allWeeks.name: Renzo Seghesio Langhe Ars Vivendi
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Elegant Piedmont red — pairs beautifully with braised meats.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Louise
allWeeks.note: Evolution track — Louise Year 1. 2019 squarely in peak window. 3 bottles remain.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Marie Grace
allWeeks.note: Méthode traditionnelle Oregon sparkling — perfect for ringing in 2027.

allWeeks.name: Trader Joe's Pauillac Diamond Reserve
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Left Bank Bordeaux — classic January brooding red.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon Don't Be a Dull Boy
allWeeks.note: Highly rated and in peak window. CT93 — Washington Cab at its finest.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Franc
allWeeks.note: In peak window. First of two Cab Francs — floral, structured.

allWeeks.name: Cascina Adelaide Barolo Fossati
allWeeks.note: Just entering its drinking window. Serious Barolo — decant 2 hours.

allWeeks.name: Cline Cellars Zinfandel Ancient Vines
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Old-vine Zin — jammy, brambly. Great with pizza on a cold night.

allWeeks.name: Drouhin Oregon Roserock Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Evolution track — Roserock Year 1. 2023 newly in window — benchmark the young expression.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2015 WR — drink up.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir 115W
allWeeks.note: End of window in 2026. Single bottle — drink now.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Hang Time
allWeeks.note: End of window this year. First of two Hang Times — open and assess.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2016 WR — 2 years past window but likely still lovely.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Louise
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. CT93 and a single bottle — end Year 1 with something worthy.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Hang Time
allWeeks.note: Second of two Hang Times — a year past window. Open alongside notes from Week 50 to compare.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: End of window. First of two 2018 WRs — drink now before further decline.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. First of two 2019 WRs — open now and take notes for the second bottle.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Lost and Found Rosé
allWeeks.note: Second of three Lost and Found Rosés. 11-year-old blend — fascinating study in aged rosé.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. First of two 2020 Dion PNs — open now and compare with the second later.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2018 WRs. Compare with Week 54 — note any difference opened a month apart.

allWeeks.name: Résonance Chardonnay Découverte Vineyard
allWeeks.note: In peak window. Rich Burgundian-style Oregon Chardonnay — great spring white.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Winemakers Reserve
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2019 WRs. Compare with Week 55 — has it held? Last chance for this label.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Chardonnay Arthur
allWeeks.note: End of window in 2028 — open while still at peak. CT91.9. Pair with roasted chicken.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Chardonnay Arthur
allWeeks.note: Past its window — open for comparison with the 2021 Arthur from Week 61. Retrospective tasting.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Marie Grace
allWeeks.note: 9-year-old Oregon méthode traditionnelle sparkling. Should be richly toasty — open with oysters.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Albariño Philips Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. Second of two — crisp summer sipper. Pair with ceviche or light seafood.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Lost and Found Rosé
allWeeks.note: Third and final Lost and Found Rosé. Compare with Week 56 — how has it evolved?

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Chardonnay Arthur
allWeeks.note: Last year of window. Pair with grilled fish or a summer butter sauce pasta.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Chardonnay Estate
allWeeks.note: In its window now. Elegant, cool-climate Oregon Chardonnay. Lovely summer white.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Peak window. First of two 2021 Dion PNs. Lighter-bodied for summer — good with salmon.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2020 Dion PNs. Last year of window — compare with the 2021 from Week 68.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Tempranillo Philips Vineyard
allWeeks.note: In its window. Washington Tempranillo — great slightly chilled on a warm evening.

allWeeks.name: LAVINEA Pinot Noir Eola-Amity Hills
allWeeks.note: Peak window. First of two LAVINEAs — elegant, mineral-driven Eola Pinot.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2021 Dion PNs. Compare with Week 68 — opened a month apart.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Chardonnay Arthur
allWeeks.note: First of three 2024 Arthurs — opening one early to benchmark the young vintage.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Tempranillo Philips Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Second Adamant Tempranillo — compare with the 2019 from Week 70.

allWeeks.name: LAVINEA Pinot Noir Eola-Amity Hills
allWeeks.note: Second of two LAVINEAs. End of summer — compare with Week 71.

allWeeks.name: Cantina Zaccagnini Montepulciano d'Abruzzo
allWeeks.note: Approaching end of window. Rustic Italian red — ideal with hearty autumn pasta.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir OG Original Vineyard
allWeeks.note: No window listed — Laurelwood District Pinot should be drinking well at 6 years. First of two OGs.

allWeeks.name: Résonance Pinot Noir Choix du Coeur Découverte Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Peak window. Single bottle — CT91.8. Burgundian producer's flagship Oregon Pinot. Decant an hour.

allWeeks.name: Jachter Pinot Noir Case Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Entering peak window. First of two Jachters — lesser-known label worth exploring.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Nalin
allWeeks.note: Entering window. First of five Nalins — red Bordeaux blend, brooding and structured.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Merlot Winemaker's Select
allWeeks.note: Entering window. First of three Merlots — Walla Walla Merlot is plush and textured.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir OG Original Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Second OG — compare 2021 vs 2022 back-to-back across Weeks 77 and 82. Great vertical.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Franc
allWeeks.note: Second of two Cab Francs (Year 1 opened the first). Floral, herbaceous — good with roasted duck.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Laurène
allWeeks.note: Annual Laurène — open 2019 (CT92.4). Compare with 2018 from Year 1 Week 36. Is it more or less open?

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Syrah Artisan
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2019 Artisan Syrahs. Last year of window — compare with Week 35. How has it evolved?

allWeeks.name: Jachter Pinot Noir Case Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Second of two Jachters. Compare with Week 79 — how has it evolved over six weeks?

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon Don't Be a Dull Boy
allWeeks.note: Entering window. First of four 2021 Cabs — compare to the CT93 2018 opened in Year 1 Week 43.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Laurène
allWeeks.note: Second of seven 2018 Laurènes. With 2018 and 2019 now open, how is the 2018 evolving?

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Old Vines
allWeeks.note: First of two 2018 Old Vines. 4 bottles in cellar — benchmarking the older vintage.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Nalin
allWeeks.note: Third of five Nalins. Track how it's evolving across the year.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Louise
allWeeks.note: Annual Louise — open 2017 (CT93.7). Compare with 2019 from Year 1 Week 40. Peak elegance.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Marie Grace
allWeeks.note: Second Marie Grace NYE — 2020 vintage this time. 7-year-old sparkling. Compare with the 2019 from Year 1.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Origine 34
allWeeks.note: Single bottle, CT92.1. New Year opener — Origine series punches well above its entry-level price.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon Don't Be a Dull Boy
allWeeks.note: Single bottle — bridge vintage between CT93 2018 and the 2021 entering window.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon Don't Be a Dull Boy
allWeeks.note: Second of four 2021 Cabs. Compare with the 2020 from last week — a nice mini-vertical.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Origine 35
allWeeks.note: Entering window. First of four Origine 35s — benchmark this vintage in the Origine lineup.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Old Vines
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2018 Old Vines. Compare with Week 89 — opened two months apart.

allWeeks.name: Drouhin Oregon Roserock Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Annual Roserock — 2022 vintage this year. Compare with the 2023 from Year 1 Week 47. Different vintage, same evolution track.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: First of two 2012 DDO PNs. 15-year-old flagship DDO — stunning tertiary development. Decant an hour.

allWeeks.name: Adamant Cellars Nalin
allWeeks.note: Fourth of five Nalins — a milestone week. Two years in. How has the Nalin evolved since Week 80?

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir
allWeeks.note: Second of two 2012 DDO PNs. Opened two weeks after Week 99 — has it changed with air?

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Wedding Tree Cuvée
allWeeks.note: Single bottle, 10 years old. CT88 — open now and enjoy whatever complexity remains.

allWeeks.name: Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir OG Original Vineyard
allWeeks.note: Single bottle — benchmark the 2023 OG. Young, vibrant Laurelwood Pinot.

allWeeks.name: Domaine Drouhin Oregon Pinot Noir Laurène
allWeeks.note: End-of-year special. 2017 Laurène (window 2033–2075) — decades of life ahead. How does it show at 10 years old?

y1.title: Spring 2026
y1.note: Urgent opens · lighter whites & rosés warming up

y1.title: Fall 2026
y1.note: Transition to reds — Washington, Italian, Oregon

y2.title: Fall 2027
y2.note: Nalin & Syrah Artisan · Laurène evolution Year 2

y1.title: Winter 2026–2027
y1.note: Big reds, Barolo, Bordeaux & evolution tracks

y2.title: Winter 2027–2028
y2.note: Louise & Roserock evolution · Old Vines vertical · Adamant Cabs

changelog.summary: Synced with CellarTracker inventory. Fixed over-counts, added urgent bottles, rescheduled to accommodate changes.

changes.description: DDO Pinot Noir Édition Limitée 2017 (CT94.5) — urgent, window closes 2027
Adamant Cellars Syrah Artisan 2019 (CT92) — first of two, replacing 2020 vintage
Adamant Cellars Syrah Artisan 2019 (CT92) — second bottle, last year of window
Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Old Vines 2018 — first of two from 4-bottle holding
Dion Vineyard Pinot Noir Old Vines 2018 — second bottle, winter comparison
Marie Grace vintage changed 2019 → 2020 (only 1 bottle of 2019 in inventory)
Roserock vintage changed 2023 → 2022 (only 1 of 2023 in inventory; 2022 serves as evolution Year 2)
Adamant Cellars Albariño 2019 dropped (no window data, low urgency)
Adamant Nalin 2021 removed (4 → 3 plan appearances, 5 bottles remain in cellar)
Adamant Merlot 2021 removed (first of two removals, 3 bottles remain)
